title: acrophorus stipellatus
intro: Acrophorus stipellatus, also known as Beach Heliotrope, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Asteraceae family. It is a shrub that grows in coastal areas, particularly in Australia, and is known for its medicinal properties. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and infections. Acrophorus stipellatus is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves, and is also used in traditional medicine to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
